title: Tethered plastic closure
abstract: The invention discloses a tethered plastic screw cap comprising a closure shell having a bottom edge and a tamper band having a top edge. Th closure shell and tamper band are linked through at least one strip having a top end linked to the closure shell and a bottom end linked to the tamper band forming a hinge. The closure includes a bottom weakness line separably linked through bottom bridges to the top edge of the tamper band and to a bottom edge of each strip, and a top weakness line separably linked through top bridges to the bottom edge of the closure shell and at least to the top edge of each strip. The hinge, at a portion nearest the strip, is configured to direct propagation of a tear away from the strip.
claims: Claims Claims The invention claimed is: 1. A tethered plastic screw cap, comprising: a closure shell having a bottom edge; a tamper band having a top edge; at least one strip having a top end linked to the closure shell and a bottom end linked to the tamper band through at least one hinge; a bottom weakness line separably linked through bottom bridges to the top edge of the tamper band and to a bottom edge of each strip; and a top weakness line separably linked through top bridges to the bottom edge of the closure shell and at least to the top edge of each strip, wherein the hinge, at a portion nearest the strip, is configured to direct propagation of a tear away from the strip. 2. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 1, wherein each weakness line has at least two side ends, which, before the cap is removed from a container, constitute side edges of the weakness lines, and after the cap is open constitute strip ends, and wherein, before the bridges are broken, at least one side edge of a weakness line is inclined related to the direction of orientation of said weakness line so that, when the cap is open and the bridges have been broken, the strip end comprises a part, corresponding to said side edge, which is not aligned with the rest of the strip, thus avoiding the propagation of the tear of the weakness line beyond said side edge. 3. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 2, wherein the side edge, which is inclined, angularly extends from 10Â° to 90Â° relative to the direction of orientation of the weakness line. 4. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 2, wherein the side edge, which is inclined, upwardly extends relative to the top weakness line. 5. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 2, wherein the side edge, which is inclined, downwardly extends relative to the bottom weakness line. 6. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 2, wherein the side edge, which is inclined, is a curved edge, so that the corresponding strip edge obtained after the bridges have been broken is curved or concave. 7. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 2, wherein the side edge, which is inclined, is a side edge of the top weakness line. 8. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 2, wherein the bottom weakness line runs on the whole periphery of the top edge of the tamper band, with the exception that it is interrupted at side edges thereof by at least one portion, which, once the cap has been opened, constitute one hinge between the tamper band and the rest of the cap and the at least one side edge, which is inclined, related to the direction of orientation of a weakness line is a side edge of the top weakness line. 9. Tethered plastic screw cap according to claim 1, wherein the hinge is positioned on the side of the strip bottom end comprises a portion, each portion having a less thickness material than the material of the hinge. 10. Tethered plastic screw stopper according to claim 9, wherein each strip being linked through a top end to the closure shell, the closure shell on the side of each strip top end comprises another portion having a less thickness material than the material of the closure shell. 11. Tethered plastic screw stopper according to claim 9, wherein each portion angularly extends from 5Â° to 30Â° relative to the hinge. 12. A tethered plastic screw stopper according to claim 1, wherein the bottom weakness line comprises at least an offset located at least at one side of the hinge, each offset being downwardly managed into the tamper band. 13. A tethered plastic screw stopper according to claim 12, wherein the bottom weakness line comprises two offsets located on either side of the hinge. 14. A tethered plastic screw stopper according to claim 12, wherein each offset vertically extends from 5 % to 30% relative to the height of the tamper band. 15. Tethered plastic screw stopper according to claim 12, wherein each offset is angularly managed along the bottom weakness line from a hinge to the nearest bottom bridge relative to the hinge. 16. Tethered plastic screw stopper according to claim 12, wherein each offset angularly extends from 5Â° to 90Â° on a side of each hinge.
